title: An Introduction to the Islamic World: 1,000 Years of History in 19 Minutes
description:
in History, Religion |   	June 2nd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

RefВӯerВӯences to Islam in major media can make it sound monoВӯlithВӯic and eterВӯnal
body:
in History, Religion |   	June 2nd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

RefВӯerВӯences to Islam in major media can make it sound monoВӯlithВӯic and eterВӯnal. But itвҖҷs actuВӯalВӯly a much younger and less uniВӯfied pheВӯnomВӯeВӯnon than many of us imagВӯine, espeВӯcialВӯly if we hapВӯpen to live outВӯside the MidВӯdle East. As a reliВӯgion, it dates back вҖңonlyвҖқ to the sevВӯenth cenВӯtuВӯry, when it was foundВӯed by the Prophet MuhamВӯmad. As an engine of large-scale civВӯiВӯlizaВӯtion, Islam took a bit longer to come into its own, and it hasВӯnвҖҷt stopped underВӯgoВӯing diviВӯsions, transВӯforВӯmaВӯtions, declines, and rebirths since. Here on Open CulВӯture, we recentВӯly feaВӯtured a video from YouTube chanВӯnel How So covВӯerВӯing 1,000 years of medieval EuroВӯpean hisВӯtoВӯry in 20 minВӯutes. The one above does the same thing for the IslamВӯic worldвҖҷs first milВӯlenВӯniВӯum, endВӯing with the rise of the Ottoman Empire.
After he unitВӯed the forВӯmerВӯly polyВӯtheВӯisВӯtic Arab tribes under his new faith, MuhamВӯmad lived for a decade longer. His death in the year 632 marked the last time that every believВӯer in Islam would have been on the same page. It was at that point that the titleВ caliph, or sucВӯcesВӯsor, was defined, and the first four caliphs after MuhamВӯmad held powВӯer for thirВӯty years, the periВӯod in which the first MusВӯlim state emerged.
The caliphate, as their terВӯriВӯtoВӯry was called, expandВӯed wideВӯly across and out of the AraВӯbiВӯan PeninВӯsuВӯla, into the terВӯriВӯtoВӯries of the ByzanВӯtine and SasВӯsanВӯian empires. SupВӯportВӯers of the earВӯly caliph Ali ibn Abi TalВӯib argued that he was the true heir to Islam, and detracВӯtors that he wasВӯnвҖҷt. EvenВӯtuВӯalВӯly, the forВӯmer group became known as the Shias, and the latВӯter as the SunВӯnis, the two sides of a schism of which pracВӯtiВӯcalВӯly everyВӯone today has heard.
LessВӯer known to the genВӯerВӯal pubВӯlic are the Umayyads, Abbasids, Buyids, and Fatimids, all of them major playВӯers in the conВӯtinВӯuВӯing expanВӯsion of Islam well into the MidВӯdle Ages. But the still-familВӯiar place names ofВ DamВӯasВӯcus, Jerusalem, BaghВӯdad, and ConВӯstanВӯtinoВӯple (or, as we know it, IstanВӯbul) are just as imporВӯtant in these chapВӯters of the stoВӯry of Islam, and withВӯout underВӯstandВӯing that reliВӯgion, itвҖҷs imposВӯsiВӯble to underВӯstand the diverse forms that civВӯiВӯlizaВӯtion has takВӯen in those places and othВӯers in the wider region of the world around them. The criВӯsis of authorВӯiВӯty that began setВӯting in after MuhamВӯmadвҖҷs death has, in some sense, perВӯsistВӯed for nearВӯly fourВӯteen cenВӯturies now, more than long enough to have become a definВӯing charВӯacВӯterВӯisВӯtic of the IslamВӯic world. What shape its sociВӯeties will take over the next milВӯlenВӯniВӯum, it would sureВӯly take a prophet to know.
RelatВӯed ConВӯtent:
The Birth and Rapid Rise of Islam, AniВӯmatВӯed (622вҖ‘1453)
500+ BeauВӯtiВӯful ManВӯuВӯscripts from the IslamВӯic World Now DigВӯiВӯtized & Free to DownВӯload
How Medieval IslamВӯic EngiВӯneerВӯing Brought Water to the AlhamВӯbraThe ComВӯplex GeomВӯeВӯtry of IslamВӯic Art & Design: A Short IntroВӯducВӯtion
The World Map That IntroВӯduced SciВӯenВӯtifВӯic MapВӯmakВӯing to the Medieval IslamВӯic World (1154 AD)
Learn IslamВӯic & IndiВӯan PhiВӯlosВӯoВӯphy with 107 Episodes of the HisВӯtoВӯry of PhiВӯlosВӯoВӯphy WithВӯout Any Gaps PodВӯcast
1,000 Years of Medieval EuroВӯpean HisВӯtoВӯry in 20 MinВӯutes
Based in Seoul,В ColВӯinВ MarshallВ writes and broadВӯcasts on cities, lanВӯguage, and culВӯture. HeвҖҷs the author of the newsletВӯterВ Books on CitiesВ as well as the booksВ н•ңкөӯ мҡ”м•Ҫ кёҲм§ҖВ (No SumВӯmaВӯrizВӯing Korea) andВ KoreВӯan Newtro.В FolВӯlow him on the social netВӯwork forВӯmerВӯly known as TwitВӯter atВ @colinmarshall.

title: Kurt Vonnegut Diagrams the Shape of All Stories in a MasterвҖҷs Thesis Rejected by U. Chicago
description:
in Design, Literature, Writing |   	June 3rd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			
вҖңWhat has been my pretВӯtiВӯest conВӯtriВӯbuВӯtion to the culВӯture?вҖқ asked Kurt
body:
in Design, Literature, Writing |   	June 3rd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			
вҖңWhat has been my pretВӯtiВӯest conВӯtriВӯbuВӯtion to the culВӯture?вҖқ asked Kurt VonВӯnegut in his autoВӯbiВӯogВӯraВӯphy Palm SunВӯday. His answer? His masterвҖҷs theВӯsis in anthroВӯpolВӯoВӯgy for the UniВӯverВӯsiВӯty of ChicaВӯgo, вҖңwhich was rejectВӯed because it was so simВӯple and looked like too much fun.вҖқ The eleВӯgant simВӯplicВӯiВӯty and playВӯfulВӯness of VonnegutвҖҷs idea is exactВӯly its endurВӯing appeal. The idea is so simВӯple, in fact, that VonВӯnegut sums the whole thing up in one eleВӯgant senВӯtence: вҖңThe funВӯdaВӯmenВӯtal idea is that stoВӯries have shapes which can be drawn on graph paper, and that the shape of a givВӯen societyвҖҷs stoВӯries is at least as interВӯestВӯing as the shape of its pots or spearВӯheads.вҖқВ In 2011, we feaВӯtured the video below of VonВӯnegut explainВӯing his theВӯoВӯry, вҖңThe Shapes of StoВӯries.вҖқ We can add to the dry wit of his lesВӯson the picВӯto-infoВӯgraphВӯic by graphВӯic designВӯer Maya EilВӯam above, which strikВӯingВӯly illusВӯtrates, with examВӯples, the varВӯiВӯous stoВӯry shapes VonВӯnegut described in his theВӯsis. (Read a conВӯdensed verВӯsion here.)
The preВӯsenВӯter who introВӯduces VonВӯnegutвҖҷs short lecВӯture tells us that вҖңhis sinВӯguВӯlar view of the world applies not just to his stoВӯries and charВӯacВӯters but to some of his theВӯoВӯries as well.вҖқ This I would affirm. When it comes to puzВӯzling out the import of a stoВӯry IвҖҷve just read, the last perВӯson I usuВӯalВӯly turn to is the author. But when it comes to what ficВӯtion is and does in genВӯerВӯal, I want to hear it from writВӯers of ficВӯtion. Some of the most endurВӯing litВӯerВӯary figВӯures are expert writВӯers on writВӯing. VonВӯnegut, a masВӯter comВӯmuВӯniВӯcaВӯtor, ranks very highВӯly among them. Does it do him a disВӯserВӯvice to conВӯdense his ideas into what look like high-res, low-readВӯabilВӯiВӯty workВӯplace safeВӯty graphВӯics? On the conВӯtrary, I think.


	

Though the design may be a litВӯtle slick for VonВӯnegutвҖҷs unapoloВӯgetВӯiВӯcalВӯly indusВӯtriВӯal approach, heвҖҷd have appreВӯciВӯatВӯed the slightВӯly corny, slightВӯly macabre boilВӯerВӯplate iconogВӯraВӯphy. His work turns a susВӯpiВӯcious eye on overВӯcomВӯpliВӯcatВӯed posВӯturВӯing and chamВӯpiВӯons unsenВӯtiВӯmenВӯtal, MidВӯwestВӯern directВӯness. VonnegutвҖҷs short, trade pubВӯliВӯcaВӯtion essay, вҖңHow to Write With Style,вҖқ is as sucВӯcinct and pracВӯtiВӯcal a stateВӯment on the subВӯject in exisВӯtence. One will encounter no more ruthВӯlessВӯly effiВӯcient list than his вҖңEight Rules for WritВӯing FicВӯtion.вҖқ But itвҖҷs in his вҖңShapes of StoВӯriesвҖқ theВӯoВӯry that I find the most insight into what ficВӯtion does, in brilВӯliantВӯly simВӯple and funВӯny ways that anyВӯone can appreВӯciВӯate.
Note: An earВӯliВӯer verВӯsion of this post appeared on our site in 2014.
RelatВӯed ConВӯtent:В 
Kurt VonВӯnegut Explains вҖңHow to Write With StyleвҖқ
The Shape of A StoВӯry: WritВӯing Tips from Kurt VonВӯnegut
Kurt VonВӯnegut Offers 8 Tips on How to Write Good Short StoВӯries

Josh Jones is a writer and musiВӯcian based in Durham, NC.В

title: The Largest Bookshelf Tour Ever Filmed: Inside a ClassicistвҖҷs 20,000-Volume Library
description:
in Books, Education |   	June 1st, 2026  2 Comments
	
			

	

If you grew up in the last few genВӯerВӯaВӯtions, chances are you didВӯnвҖҷt get much of an
body:
in Books, Education |   	June 1st, 2026  2 Comments
	
			

	

If you grew up in the last few genВӯerВӯaВӯtions, chances are you didВӯnвҖҷt get much of an eduВӯcaВӯtion, if any, in Latin or ancient Greek. One long-made arguВӯment for phasВӯing them out of curВӯricВӯuВӯla in EngВӯlish-speakВӯing counВӯtries holds that room must be made for SpanВӯish, ManВӯdarin, and othВӯerВ lanВӯguages actuВӯalВӯly used at scale in the modВӯern world. NowaВӯdays, when even those classВӯes face the presВӯsure of extincВӯtion,В advoВӯcaВӯcy for clasВӯsiВӯcal lanВӯguages exudes an ever stronger conВӯtrarВӯiВӯan appeal. вҖңDeadвҖқ though they may be, they also live on through not just the Romance lanВӯguages, but also the mighty hegeВӯmon known as EngВӯlish. Indeed, it makes sense to ask whether an AngloВӯphone withВӯout knowlВӯedge of Latin or Greek truВӯly underВӯstands his own native tongue.
Nor, accordВӯing to clasВӯsiВӯcist David ButВӯterВӯfield, can one learn Latin withВӯout havВӯing any Greek. GetВӯting a hanВӯdle on both of those lanВӯguages and their surВӯvivВӯing body of texts isnвҖҷt just the work of a lifeВӯtime; it also fills a house, as eviВӯdenced by the two-and-a-half-hour video tour of ButВӯterВӯfieldВӯвҖҷs perВӯsonВӯal library above. (The subВӯseВӯquent two hours conВӯtain ButВӯterВӯfieldВӯвҖҷs introВӯducВӯtions to a selecВӯtion of parВӯticВӯuВӯlar volВӯumes from his many shelves.) YoutuВӯber TimВӯoВӯthy KenВӯny has preВӯviВӯousВӯly uploaded quite a few such videos on the colВӯlecВӯtions of seriВӯous bibВӯlioВӯphiles, but this one he describes as the largest ever attemptВӯed, includВӯing the comВӯplete Loeb ClasВӯsiВӯcal Library, I TatВӯti RenaisВӯsance Library, and Pauly-WisВӯsowa encyВӯcloВӯpeВӯdias.
Yet accordВӯing to ButВӯterВӯfield himВӯself, a young man by the stanВӯdards of his proВӯfesВӯsion and speВӯcialВӯty, heвҖҷs still got a lot of colВӯlectВӯing to do. HeвҖҷs only about 80 perВӯcent of the way to a full set of Oxford UniВӯverВӯsiВӯty PressвҖҷ Very Short IntroВӯducВӯtions, a series through which IвҖҷve been gradВӯuВӯalВӯly makВӯing my own way in recent years. HavВӯing found that its books offer вҖңa realВӯly good view of whatВӯevВӯer the topВӯic or perВӯson is,вҖқ he decidВӯed to вҖңcolВӯlect all the volВӯumes that interВӯestВӯed me. And that emerged to be more than I thought, because I am interВӯestВӯed in almost everyВӯthing.вҖқ But with all of us, no matВӯter how broadВӯly curiВӯous, some of his interВӯests are stronger than othВӯers, as one might expect from a man with the patience to amass a great amount of manВӯuВӯals for writВӯing Greek and Latin prose and verse made for schoolВӯboys (and, often, conВӯtainВӯing their dooВӯdles).
After spendВӯing a couВӯple of decades at CamВӯbridge, ButВӯterВӯfield crossed the Atlantic to go from one of the oldВӯest instiВӯtuВӯtions of highВӯer eduВӯcaВӯtion to one of the very newest. HeвҖҷs now Provost of and ProВӯfesВӯsor of Latin at RalВӯston ColВӯlege in SavanВӯnah, GeorВӯgia, which received its first cohort of stuВӯdents in 2022. With its masВӯterвҖҷs degree proВӯgram closeВӯly focused on ancient, medieval and modВӯern litВӯerВӯaВӯture and art conВӯsidВӯered founВӯdaВӯtionВӯal to WestВӯern civВӯiВӯlizaВӯtion, it seems like the kind of instiВӯtuВӯtion designed to attract someВӯone like ButВӯterВӯfield, who was already winВӯning prizes for his library in or shortВӯly after his colВӯlege days. вҖңI canвҖҷt see myself relaxВӯing until I have accuВӯmuВӯlatВӯed around 10,000 books,вҖқ he said in a 2008 interВӯview. His home, as capВӯtured in KenВӯnyвҖҷs video, now conВӯtains douВӯble that amount, but theВ thuВӯmos clearВӯly hasВӯnвҖҷt desertВӯed him just yet.
RelatВӯed conВӯtent:

The 321 Books in David FosВӯter WallaceвҖҷs PerВӯsonВӯal Library: From Blood MeridВӯiВӯan to ConВӯfesВӯsions of an UnlikeВӯly BodyВӯbuilder

Based in Seoul,В ColВӯinВ MarshallВ writes and broadВӯcasts on cities, lanВӯguage, and culВӯture. HeвҖҷs the author of the newsletВӯterВ Books on CitiesВ as well as the booksВ н•ңкөӯ мҡ”м•Ҫ кёҲм§ҖВ (No SumВӯmaВӯrizВӯing Korea) andВ KoreВӯan Newtro.В FolВӯlow him on the social netВӯwork forВӯmerВӯly known as TwitВӯter atВ @colinmarshall.

title: The First Live Performance of NirvanaвҖҷs вҖңSmells Like Teen SpiritвҖқ (1991)
description:
in Music |   	June 2nd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

ItвҖҷs almost 35 years ago now that NirvanaвҖҷs video for вҖңSmells Like Teen SpirВӯitвҖқ debuted on
body:
in Music |   	June 2nd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

ItвҖҷs almost 35 years ago now that NirvanaвҖҷs video for вҖңSmells Like Teen SpirВӯitвҖқ debuted on MTVвҖҷs 120 MinВӯutes and, for betВӯter or worse, inauВӯguВӯratВӯed the grunge era. The video (below) arrived as a shock and a thrill to a genВӯerВӯaВӯtion too young to rememВӯber punk and sick of the steady stream of cheesy corВӯpoВӯrate dance music and hair metВӯal that charВӯacВӯterВӯized the late-80s. For everyВӯone outВӯside the small SeatВӯtle scene that nurВӯtured them and the tape-tradВӯing kids in the know, the band seemed to arrive out of nowhere as a total angst-ridВӯden packВӯage, and the MTV video, by first-time direcВӯtor Samuel BayВӯer, seemed bracВӯingВӯly anarВӯchic and raw at the time.
But a look at the first live perВӯforВӯmance of вҖңTeen SpirВӯitвҖқ (above) makes it seem pretВӯty tame by comВӯparВӯiВӯson. The videoвҖҷs a litВӯtle grainy and low-res, which suits the song just fine. Live, вҖңTeen SpirВӯitвҖҷsвҖқ disВӯturbВӯing underВӯtones are more proВӯnounced, its quiВӯet-loud dynamВӯics more forceВӯful, and the enerВӯgy of the crowd is real, not the thrashВӯing around of a bunch of teenage extras. Not a cheerВӯleader in sight, but I think this would have grabbed me more than the pep ralВӯly-riot-themed MTV video did when it debuted a few months latВӯer. Despite their anti-corВӯpoВӯrate stance, NirВӯvana was a casuВӯalВӯty of their own sucВӯcess, eatВӯen up by the machinВӯery they despised. Their best moments are still the unscriptВӯed and unpreВӯdictable. For conВӯtrast, zip back to 1991 and watch the MTV video below.


	

Note: An earВӯliВӯer verВӯsion of this post appeared on our site in 2012.

title: Pacific Surf Takes Center Stage in Craig Hubbard's Dreamy Photos
description: When Craig Hubbard moved from Brooklyn to Venice Beach in 2013, he had an established creative career as an animator and comic book colorist, but
body:
When Craig Hubbard moved from Brooklyn to Venice Beach in 2013, he had an established creative career as an animator and comic book colorist, but it had been a long time since he had picked up a camera. The golden hour hues of the West CoastвҖҷs legendary sunsets reacquainted him with lens-based work, and he began documenting the areas he frequented in his spare time. вҖңAs an avid surfer and former skater,В I gravitate toward skateparks and water,вҖқ he tells Colossal. And with the ocean, of course, come the waves.
Venice Beach is a funky, coastal Los Angeles neighborhood that has retained its laid-back, surf-loving vibe despite new developments. Surfers await swells in areas like the Breakwater and the Venice Beach Pier, and Hubbard heads out with his own board and his camera. вҖңDusk, dawn, and fog banks activate my senses,вҖқ he says. Tapping into his enthusiasm for graphic design, he focuses on bold outlines and forms, high contrast, and the energy of directionality and motion. вҖңNature ultimately does the heavy lifting, though,вҖқ he adds.

HubbardвҖҷs photos are ethereal and cinematic, with surfers and wave crests illuminated by the early morning sun or backdropped by the marine layer. Sometimes the intense spray, curl, shoulder, or lip become the sole subjects of the portraits. вҖңThe water is the muse and artist,вҖқ Hubbard recently told an interviewer. вҖңIвҖҷm just a biased translator and documentarian. Lastly, my ego relaxes in the ocean; the need to peacock recedes. This is where my best work comes fromвҖ”or favorite, I should say.вҖқ 
Follow HubbardвҖҷs work on Instagram and see some of his videos on YouTube.

schema.headline: Pacific Surf Takes Center Stage in Craig Hubbard's Dreamy Photos

title: An Interactive Sculpture by Wade and Leta Celebrates the Sun-Bleached Australian Landscape
description: In SydneyвҖҷs Circular Quay, a 6.5-meter-tall installation spins, twirls, and totters amid a public thoroughfare. Titled вҖңThere, Now, Here,вҖқ the kin
body:
In SydneyвҖҷs Circular Quay, a 6.5-meter-tall installation spins, twirls, and totters amid a public thoroughfare. Titled вҖңThere, Now, Here,вҖқ the kinetic artwork is by the Brooklyn-based duo Wade and Leta and is in almost constant motion, thanks to wind, motors, and willing participants hopping on a see-saw.
With black and white stripes alongside a more muted palette, the colors of the playground-style project reference Dorothea MackellarвҖҷs beloved poem вҖңMy Country,вҖқ which professes her devotion to the Australian landscape and what she dubs the вҖңsunburnt country.вҖқ Harnessing the washed-out tones of a sun-bleached environment, the artists present their signature bold works in more subtle hues, as if the pieces have been baking under the light for years.

A sonic component created by Josh Burgess accompanies the sculpture and can be manipulated by the public through accessible controls. вҖңIf one were to listen closely, they can hear the rush of water on the rocks, the dings of the light rail, the crosswalk signal, and most importantly, the local wildlife,вҖқ the artists say. вҖңOur favorite piece is a nod to the вҖҳbush doofвҖҷ using the sounds of a lyrebird as the structure.вҖқ
вҖңThere, Now, HereвҖқ is the pairвҖҷs first public work in Australia and part of the annual light and music festival Vivid Sydney. Find more on Wade and LetaвҖҷs Instagram.

schema.headline: An Interactive Sculpture by Wade and Leta Celebrates the Sun-Bleached Australian Landscape

title: Spectral Fronds and Flowers Comprise Elegant Animals in Molly Devlin's Paintings
description:
All images courtesy of the artist, shared with permission
June 2, 2026
ArtNature
Kate Mothes

With bodies composed of ghostly ferns, flowers, and fun
body:
All images courtesy of the artist, shared with permission
June 2, 2026
ArtNature
Kate Mothes

With bodies composed of ghostly ferns, flowers, and fungi, Molly DevlinвҖҷs fantastical and ethereal acrylic portraits invite us into a dreamy woodland realm. Her works tap into the beauty and resilience of living creatures, from a white bear cloaked in translucent butterflies to a diminutive mouse composed of different lifecycle phases of a dandelion. Part fauna and part flora, each elegant animal is a reminder of natureвҖҷs interconnectedness.
Devlin is currently working toward a solo exhibition opening in early August at Corey Helford Gallery in Los Angeles. SheвҖҷs also finishing up a mural in collaboration with S.V. Williams along the American River in Sacramento. See more on Instagram.
